extra.message: The TLS server configuration clears ssl.OP_NO_SSLv3, which can re-enable the obsolete SSLv3 protocol if supported by the runtime.

cwe: CWE-326: Inadequate Encryption Strength

extra.message: The TLS server configuration clears ssl.OP_NO_COMPRESSION, enabling TLS compression and exposing connections to compression side-channel attacks.

cwe: CWE-326: Inadequate Encryption Strength

extra.message: SESSIONID cookies are stored in a class-level SimpleCookie shared by all handler instances, so one request can retain or overwrite authentication state for another request.

cwe: CWE-488: Exposure of Data Element to Wrong Session

extra.message: The full HTTP request line is written to logs, including query-string credentials such as the /login username and password parameters.

cwe: CWE-532: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File

extra.message: The request path is concatenated into open() for binary static files without normalization or directory restriction, allowing ../ path traversal to read files outside the web root.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: The request path is concatenated into open() for text static files without normalization or directory restriction, allowing ../ path traversal to read files outside the web root.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: The BDR HTTP method passes the user-controlled URL path to subprocess.check_output() with shell=True, allowing arbitrary OS command injection when risk level 3 is enabled.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: Session tokens are generated with random.sample(), a non-cryptographic PRNG, making SESSIONID values predictable.

cwe: CWE-338: Use of Cryptographically Weak Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)

extra.message: Unhandled exception messages are converted to response content and sent to clients via send_error(), leaking internal error details.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: SESSIONID cookies are sent without the HttpOnly attribute, allowing client-side scripts to read session cookies.

cwe: CWE-1004: Sensitive Cookie Without HttpOnly Flag

extra.message: SESSIONID cookies are sent without the Secure attribute, allowing transmission over unencrypted HTTP when the application is not strictly HTTPS-only.

cwe: CWE-614: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without Secure Attribute

extra.message: The response explicitly disables browser XSS filtering with X-XSS-Protection: 0.

cwe: CWE-16: Configuration

extra.message: The Content-Security-Policy allows all sources and 'unsafe-inline', weakening protection against script injection.

cwe: CWE-16: Configuration

extra.message: The id query parameter is concatenated directly into a SELECT statement passed to cursor.execute(), allowing SQL injection.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: The unauthenticated /users handler selects user records by a user-controlled id without an authorization check, allowing arbitrary user data access.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The /users query retrieves the session column and renders query results to the response, exposing session identifiers to unauthenticated users.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

extra.message: SQL query result values are inserted into HTML table cells without escaping; an attacker can inject HTML/JavaScript through controlled database or UNION SELECT values.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The msg query parameter is returned as page content without HTML escaping, allowing reflected cross-site scripting.

extra.message: Stored guestbook comments are rendered into HTML table cells without escaping, allowing persistent cross-site scripting.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: The path query parameter is embedded inside a JavaScript string without escaping, allowing reflected script injection.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The admin page builds privileged content and only performs a client-side redirect when no cookie is present, so unauthenticated users still receive the protected content.
